summary: Iteration 4 — hybrid Claude Sonnet 4.5 vision + GPT Image 1 pipeline + new landing-page cluttered before images. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass in 41.6s, including a live end-to-end free submission whose `after` is a >1KB GPT Image 1 PNG that also persists via GET /api/submissions/{id}. Robustness verified: a malformed image_b64 returns 200 with empty `after` (no 500). Regression on /api/plans, /api/checkout/session (plus + premium accepted, free rejected 400), /api/submissions/{id}/pdf 402 for free / 404 for unknown id all pass. Frontend: landing page renders the three NEW before images (substrings `iynilrc8_cluttered_garage`, `9qy6to10_cluttered_closet`, `z2z93f26_cluttered_laundry_room` all present in ). Gallery filter buttons (all/garage/closet/laundry) work, FAQ accordion opens, free CTA navigates to /upload/free. End-to-end upload→budget-modal→/result/{id} flow works and the result page renders a photorealistic organized-room AFTER image generated by GPT Image 1. All review_request criteria met.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:445 uses asyncio.gather without return_exceptions=True — if generate_after_image raises (instead of returning None) all photos in the batch fail. generate_after_image currently catches all exceptions so this is fine in practice, but adding return_exceptions=True would harden it.

server.py:445 fans out N concurrent GPT Image 1 calls. For Premium (4 photos) this is 4 parallel image generations costing ~$0.10–0.20/submission and ~30–60s each; consider a semaphore or sequential fallback to avoid rate-limit bursts.
_describe_room() truncates Claude output to 700 chars but does not validate format; if Claude returns refusal text it will still be fed into the prompt. Low risk but worth a sanity check.

Stale tests test_paid_plan_rejected_without_session / _with_unpaid_session in test_flowspace_api.py remain (paid bypass still in effect per iter3) — recommend deleting or marking xfail to keep the suite green.

Backend currently stores every base64 before+after PNG inside Mongo documents (potentially several MB per submission). Long-term: move binaries to object storage or GridFS to keep documents under the 16MB cap once medium-quality GPT Image 1 outputs grow.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: One real free submission was made through the API and UI with TEST_iter4_* prefixed emails. Each created a real GPT Image 1 PNG (~30–45s).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter4 verified the Gemini→GPT Image 1 migration end-to-end with live API calls. test_iter4_gpt_image.py performs an actual hybrid-pipeline submission and asserts the returned `after` decodes to >1KB; uses TIMEOUT_LONG=240s. Free plan UI flow on /upload/free shows the budget modal even for free (modal opens on click of 'Transform my room'), and /result/{id} renders the after image as a data:image base64. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is wired correctly for both anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) and OpenAIImageGeneration (gpt-image-1, quality=medium).
